title: Chapter Eighty-Eight: Protectors
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He wanted her alive.

If she died here, the mission collapsed. But alive and humiliated before Wuchang's dignitaries -- that was a picture worth painting.

"What should I do with you," he said through the mask. The veins on his hand looked ominous against her white neck. "Lowly Namgung surname. However, being a name spread wide, through you the vitality of our sect could be announced."

The Wuchang martial artists understood. A Blood Flame Sect noble, come to prove his sect's survival by subduing a Namgung scion. Anyone could follow that logic.

"How about making you a servant? To wash my feet."

The demon mask's gaping mouth seemed to contemplate its prey.

"You'll need my permission to operate true qi," he said. "It's the divine power of blood technique."

"How dare you!" Namgung Mi's brow twisted. Not despair -- fierce indignation. Something impossible in her life had occurred, and rage was all she had left. "How dare you!"

He sealed her further. The explosion sounds of energy waves hitting blood points rang like drums bursting.

That was when the main gate opened.

A cool wind swept in. Straightforward energy waves. Clean and certain.

"A blood demon has nested at the site of an evil sect's extermination." A young man in a blue martial robe stood in the doorway. The *Desolate* character was embroidered beside his shoulder. His straight nose and small closed lips resembled Namgung Mi's -- blood kin, unmistakably. "I came to offer congratulatory drinks to my sister."

"Desolate Fortress!"

"White Qilin! It's Namgung's White Qilin!"

Namgung Hwa-sin had arrived with Yeon-shin the moment the banquet began. He had heard every word -- the praise for Namgung, the insults toward Desolate Sect, the crowd's cowardice dressed as conviction. Now he stepped forward, and behind him the crowd's faces bloomed with relief.

*'He created an opportunity,'* Hwa-sin thought, watching his companion's steady breathing through the demon mask. *'It's a favour. Must succeed no matter what.'*

"Blood demon. Step away."

"Another Namgung mongrel? Step away? Your words are laughable."

The masked figure threw Namgung Mi to the ground like refuse. Drew his sword. The bearing that flowed from the draw was desolate sword -- the same style as the man he faced.

STEP. STEP.

They walked toward each other like men taking strolls. Swords lowered. The air between them compressed with each stride. Ten steps. Eight. Five.

BOOM!

They blurred. Two swords caught sunlight and distorted it into white streams. Red and blue robes brushed past each other.

CLANG--!

Namgung Hwa-sin stopped. The sword cry that rang from his blade was almost enough to break it. He had intended to match blows -- but the trajectory and power were different from their competition sparring. Lightning Genius had grown.

*'He completely incorporated his raised true qi into the sword path. The concentrated power was different.'*

The demon mask sheathed his sword with the bearing of a man who owned the ground he stood on.

"Worthy of mentioning the Divine Sword Group Leader," he said. "Desolate Fortress. Wait and see. The blood lineage nobles will return."

He walked out through the main gate without turning his back. Arrogant. Unhurried.

"Bugs," he said, and was gone.

---

In the silence that followed, Namgung Hwa-sin turned to his half-sister. She had risen from the ground and stood proudly, as if she had never been thrown. Her eyes burned toward the gate -- not at him, but at the direction the demon mask had gone.

*Obsession,* Hwa-sin thought. *I've never seen that look on her face.*

"Our side will take charge of your safety," he said. "We guarantee protection until the martial matches."

"What?"

"Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius is with me. With Young Hero Jeong and I, you can safely reach South Zhili."

Behind Namgung Mi, the faces of Wuchang's martial artists shifted like wind through grass.

"Protecting an opponent promised to life-and-death matches?" one whispered.

"Truly righteous and fair."

"Desolate Fortress -- they spoke of civilian welfare..."

The fruits of the battle for justification were bearing. They could behead the Namgung family's leadership now without minding the people's eyes.

---

High above the marketplace, in the highest tree in Wuchang, a woman sat on a thick branch with one leg dangling. Her black silk hair moved in the gentle wind. Her red pupils -- bright as demon jade -- remained fixed on the boy below.

She watched him leave the courtyard with movements like drifting leaves. Watched him remove the mask and the pure blood robe in an empty space where no one could see.

"My little Grand Teacher," the Seventh Apostle murmured.

Her soliloquy had grown longer lately. Perhaps because her time standing alone in the murim had lengthened. Or perhaps she had always been this way -- born inherently noble and alone.

*Heung.*

She laughed through her nose.

"You are sweet."

She had engraved his voice in her ears -- the way Demon Roaring Blood Technique's true qi wrapped around his words, the self-centred bearing, the look of him in the pure blood robe as if it had been made for no one else.

Her whole body felt like melting.

Her right eye swept over every detail of him with gleaming red light. His breathing. His posture. The way he folded the robe and set it aside.

Her heart felt like it would tear.

*Those things are completely yours, my little Grand Teacher.*

"What should I do to make you accept me?" she whispered.

*Wouldn't it be fine to cut off all his limbs and take him?*

She shook her head immediately. No. He was an existence that must not be ruined by wicked desires. He had to create divine techniques that would illuminate the world like a comet.

"Show me an opening," she said, and lightly licked her lips.

---
